content.claim: Children who pass competitive entrance exams into 6/8-year gimnázium programs, typically drawn disproportionately from higher-SES and urban families, gain access to schools with stronger peer composition and higher short-term value-added, while students remaining in standard primary schools face diminished-quality residual peer groups.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Long-run individual benefit to selected students less clearly established than short-term relative advantage
Compositional finding based on synthesis of multiple studies rather than single direct measurement
Peer composition effects may reflect selection bias rather than pure peer influence
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
